title: Pancreatic Insulin Content by Acid-Ethanol Extraction
author: Ed Leiter
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.3jjgkkn
materials: Homogenize tissue (I use a Polytron homogenizer).
Divide Insulin content ng/ml by Protein content µg/ml.

steps.title: ¼ - ½ of the pancreas...
steps.instruction: ¼ - ½ of the pancreas is placed into 5 ml Acid-Ethanol (1.5% HCl in 70% EtOH) in a 15 ml conical vial.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2000 rpm 15 min at 4ºC (Sorvall RT6000)
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2000 rpm 15 min at 4ºC (Sorvall RT6000).

steps.title: Transfer aqueous solution to a new 15 ml conical vial
steps.instruction: Transfer aqueous solution to a new 15 ml conical vial.

steps.title: Neutralize 100 µl of Acid-Ethanol extract...
steps.instruction: Neutralize 100 µl of Acid-Ethanol extract with 100 µl 1M Tris pH 7.5.

steps.title: Dilute further (1:100, 1:1000, or 1:5000...
steps.instruction: Dilute further (1:100, 1:1000, or 1:5000 depending upon the strain) in Insulin ELISA sample diluent.

steps.title: Run diluted sample on Insulin ELISA (Exocell)
steps.instruction: Run diluted sample on Insulin ELISA (Exocell). Calculate ng/ml with appropriate dilution factor.

steps.title: Run 20 µl of the neutralized...
steps.instruction: Run 20 µl of the neutralized solution in a Bradford Assay (250 µl Coomassie Blue Reagent, Thermo Scientific) against a standard curve. Calculate µg/ml with appropriate dilution factor.
